prompt: Explain photosynthesis to a curious adult non-scientist in around 150 words. Avoid a bulleted list.
response: Photosynthesis is the remarkable process that plants, algae, and some bacteria use to capture energy from sunlight and turn it into chemical energy stored in sugars. It mainly happens in the leaves, inside cells packed with tiny structures called chloroplasts, which contain the green pigment chlorophyll. When sunlight strikes chlorophyll, it energizes the plant to combine carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil. This reaction produces glucose, a simple sugar that serves as food, and releases oxygen as a byproduct. Essentially, photosynthesis lets plants convert the sun’s energy into a usable form, fueling not just their own growth but also providing oxygen and food for almost all life on Earth. In this way, photosynthesis supports nearly every ecosystem, making it one of the most crucial biological processes on our planet.
